_comment: Hidden review list for /draft.html and /draftnewsletter.html. Articles in review/finalisation pipeline. Edit this file to add/remove drafts. The 'file' field is the URL relative to the site root.

drafts.title: The Rational Is Your Sous-Chef: A Sole-Chef Operating Manual
drafts.lede: You are alone in the galley. Twelve guests, six crew, three services a day, and one body. The Rational on the wall cost €18,000 and the previous chef used four of its programmes. Meanwhile the box is sitting there with a probe, a steam injector, and a memory bank — waiting to be your second pair of hands.

drafts.title: Port Call Split: The Dalmatian Hub Playbook
drafts.lede: Split is the busiest charter hub in the Adriatic and one of the few Mediterranean cities where a serious daily fish market still sits inside the old town walls. The Ribarnica at 06:30, the Pazar at the Palace wall, METRO for the bulk run, Pelješac for the wine — and the Sućidar pazar at Plokite, neighbourhood butchers, and the Stobreč fish shop the locals actually use.

drafts.title: Build Your Own Recipe Library: Cookbooks, Open Archives, and AI
drafts.lede: You own thirty cookbooks. You've bookmarked hundreds of pages. When you need the best banana bread at 6 AM before a charter, you still Google it. There is a better way. Build a personal recipe library from the books you trust, load the open archives that already exist, and use AI to retrieve from your own shelf — not the entire internet.

drafts.title: The Locker: Deep Pantry Guides for Chefs Who Stock Like an Arsenal
drafts.lede: You do not need to know what miso is. You need to know which miso, from which producer, and why it matters when there are forty options and you are ordering online from a marina in Mallorca with three hours before the delivery cutoff.

drafts.title: Signal Fire: One Chef, One Idea That Changes Everything
drafts.lede: This is not a chef profile. It is the dissection of a single idea — one technique, one principle, one obsession — that happened to come from someone who spent their life in kitchens. The chef is the vehicle. The idea is the payload.
